claim: More useful tokens outperform fewer tokens; the key metric is token usefulness, not raw token count—visual specs and HTML specs provide more useful context than plain markdown
actionable: When writing specs for agents, prioritize token usefulness over minimalism; use HTML, visual specs, or images to communicate requirements more effectively even if it increases token count.
source_title: Pi Coding Agent Observability: HTML Specs with Gemini 3.5 Flash and GPT Image 2

claim: Agent observability (measuring token usage, execution time, cost per agent run) is essential; without measurement, you cannot improve agent performance or optimize cost
actionable: Implement agent observability dashboards that track: cost per run, tokens per second, cost per token, and event counts; use this data to compare spec types and model choices.
source_title: Pi Coding Agent Observability: HTML Specs with Gemini 3.5 Flash and GPT Image 2

claim: Gemini 3.5 Flash offers exceptional cost-to-performance ratio for product agents; despite higher pricing than expected, the intelligence output per token justifies the cost
actionable: When selecting models for agent workflows, optimize for cost-per-intelligence not cost-per-token; Gemini 3.5 Flash is strong for multimodal agent work despite premium pricing.
source_title: Pi Coding Agent Observability: HTML Specs with Gemini 3.5 Flash and GPT Image 2

claim: Visual specs (Vspecs) combining HTML with embedded images are superior to plain markdown or HTML-only specs for agent planning; images communicate intent faster than text
actionable: Adopt visual specs for all agent planning; embed images via tools like GPT Image 2 to show UI mockups, architecture diagrams, and data flows directly in plans.
source_title: Pi Coding Agent Observability: HTML Specs with Gemini 3.5 Flash and GPT Image 2

claim: Building 'product-focused agents' that argue counter-positions (steelman arguments) requires careful prompt engineering to avoid default sycophancy where models lean into user agreement
actionable: When building devil's advocate or counter-argument agents, explicitly instruct models to construct the strongest opposing case and resist agreement bias; measure output quality by argument strength.
source_title: Pi Coding Agent Observability: HTML Specs with Gemini 3.5 Flash and GPT Image 2

claim: Agentic engineering success requires three steps: use more tokens (step 1), generate value from tokens (step 2), capture revenue from that value (step 3)—token maxing alone is not a strategy
actionable: Structure agent systems with three layers: token optimization, value generation (correct tool use, good output), and revenue capture; measure progress at each layer separately.
source_title: Pi Coding Agent Observability: HTML Specs with Gemini 3.5 Flash and GPT Image 2

claim: Multimodal models (especially Gemini) have compounding advantages for agent specs; they excel on multimodal benchmarks (MMLU) making them ideal for text+image specifications
actionable: Prefer Gemini models for agent systems that use visual specs; their multimodal performance on benchmarks translates to better spec comprehension and execution.
source_title: Pi Coding Agent Observability: HTML Specs with Gemini 3.5 Flash and GPT Image 2
